steps.title: Calculate Percent Increase from 2012 to 2013
steps.content:
From 2012 to 2013, subscriptions increased from 5,600 to 5,880.
Use the formula: percent increase = $\frac{\text{New - Old}}{\text{Old}} \quad 100$.
$$\frac{5880 - 5600}{5600} \quad 100 = \frac{280}{5600} \quad 100 = 5\%.$$

steps.title: Relate 2013 to 2014 Increase
steps.content:
The 2012–2013 increase (5%) was double the 2013–2014 increase.
So 2013–2014 increase is $5\% \quad \frac{1}{2} = 2.5\%$.

steps.title: Apply 2.5% Increase to 2013 Value
steps.content:
Calculate 2.5% of 5,880 and add to 5,880:
$$5880 \quad 0.025 = 147\quad\text{(since 2.5% = 0.025)}$$
$5880 + 147 = 6027$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
